answers.body:
Set management.observations.annotations.enabled to true, and NewSpan annotation will be processed.

https://docs.spring.io/spring-boot/reference/actuator/observability.html#actuator.observability.annotations

I could suggest few options how to create a new span.

I personally prefer the 1st one because it separates configuration from business logic the most and applies globally.
But it is less flexible than the 2nd, also 2nd does require to change your configs a lot.

1) Configure Executor bean so it creates a new span

Instead of ContextExecutorService.wrap use TaskDecorator.
And instead of Executors.newCachedThreadPool() use ThreadPoolTaskExecutor() in order to set TaskDecorator.
So you need to change yours config to something like:

@Configuration
@EnableAsync
public class MicrometerConfig implements AsyncConfigurer {

    private final Tracer tracer;

    public MicrometerConfig(Tracer tracer) {
        this.tracer = tracer;
    }

    private TaskDecorator taskDecorator() {
        return runnable -> ContextSnapshotFactory
            .builder()
            .build()
            .captureAll()
            .wrap(wrapInNewSpan(runnable, tracer));
    }

    private Runnable wrapInNewSpan(Runnable runnable, Tracer tracer) {
        return () -> {
            Span span = tracer.nextSpan().name("new-span").start();
            try (Tracer.SpanInScope scope = tracer.withSpan(span)) {
                runnable.run();
            } finally {
                span.end();
            }
        };
    }

    @Override
    public Executor getAsyncExecutor() {
        return ThreadPoolTaskExecutor executor = new ThreadPoolTaskExecutorBuilder()
            .taskDecorator(taskDecorator())
            .build();
        executor.initialize();
        return executor;
    }
}

2) Use annotation @NewSpan

  • Mark the @Async methods with @NewSpan
  • Add config management.observations.annotations.enabled=true to application.yaml
  • Add dependency org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-aop

title: Configuring Micrometer to Automatically Create New Span on Async
body:
I am trying to get Spring Boot 3.2 to work with trace/span ids in a similar fashion to the way Spring Cloud Sleuth works in Spring Boot 2.

I have come up with the following configuration class:

@Configuration
class MicrometerConfig implements AsyncConfigurer {
    public static Executor executor() {
        return ContextExecutorService.wrap(ForkJoinPool.commonPool(), contextSnapshotFactory::captureAll);
    }

    private static final ContextSnapshotFactory contextSnapshotFactory = ContextSnapshotFactory.builder()
        .build();

    @Override
    public Executor getAsyncExecutor() {
        return ContextExecutorService.wrap(Executors.newCachedThreadPool(), contextSnapshotFactory::captureAll);
    }
}

This is working for @Async methods, but does not create new spans for new threads. I tried putting a @NewSpan annotation on my @Async method, but this causes my method to not run asynchronously anymore.

I also tried putting a manual new span in form my async executor

@Override
public Executor getAsyncExecutor() {
    Span span = tracer.nextSpan();
    span.start();
    try (Tracer.SpanInScope ignored = tracer.withSpan(span)) {
        log.info("NEWSPAN");
        return ContextExecutorService.wrap(Executors.newCachedThreadPool(), contextSnapshotFactory::captureAll);
    }
}

While the log.info("NEWSPAN") does produce a new span in the log, the actual method is still producing the outside span id. What am I missing?
